dialogueChunks.intro: The council heard the evidence. The founding families are furious, but they can't deny the numbers. The siphoning was systematic and documented.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} brokered a new accord: wages adjusted, community funds restored, and a public accounting system so nobody can skim again. The founding family's son who struck the worker publicly apologized. Whether he meant it is debatable. Whether it matters is less so.
dialogueChunks.outro: The settlement won't change overnight, {player_name}. Power doesn't redistribute quietly. But the workers have a seat at the table now, and that's something they didn't have yesterday. {quest_ally} says to tell you the first fair payroll goes out next week.

playerResponses.text: A seat at the table is where change starts. Good luck to them.
Keep watching the books. Corruption doesn't stay gone on its own.

dialogueChunks.intro: The charter was clear. The taxes were illegal. The landowner's own guards opened the storehouse doors when they saw the founding document.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The families got their harvest back. Every bushel. {target_npc} supervised the distribution personally. The landowner hasn't been seen since: some say he left in the night, others say he's locked in his estate drafting new schemes. {quest_ally} organized a watch rotation to make sure the storehouse stays public.
dialogueChunks.outro: A piece of paper with old words on it broke a tyrant's grip, {player_name}. That's the thing about legitimate authority: it only works as long as nobody reads the fine print. You read it.

playerResponses.text: The law was always on their side. They just needed someone to prove it.
The landowner will try again. Stay vigilant.
Next time, don't wait for an outsider. Fight for your own.

dialogueChunks.intro: The trade council ruled unanimously. The guild examinations are suspended pending a complete overhaul. Three guild masters have been stripped of their positions.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The expelled apprentice was reinstated with a formal apology. {target_npc} says fourteen other apprentices who had been failed unfairly are being reassessed. {quest_ally} is drafting new examination standards with input from every skill level, not just the top. It's messy and loud and exactly what reform looks like.
dialogueChunks.outro: The guild won't forgive you easily, {player_name}. But the apprentices who can finally earn what they deserve: they'll remember. Sometimes the inferior just needed a fair chance, not a revolution. You gave them one.

playerResponses.text: Fair chances shouldn't require someone breaking the system. Fix it properly.
The guild masters got off easy. But at least the system changes.
